steps.title: Identify the core contrast
steps.content: The energy ladder model reduces fuel choice to primarily one factor: household income. Heltberg's study shows the reality is more complex—fuel choice is influenced by several factors, including local prices. The author calls the model “reductive” because it oversimplifies a multifaceted phenomenon to a single cause.

steps.title: Match the logic to the choices
steps.content: Choice D directly captures this: the energy ladder “attributes household fuel choice primarily to a single characteristic of households,” while Heltberg found “multiple circumstances can affect household fuel choice.” The other three either confuse what the energy ladder claims, shift focus to displacement rather than the number of influencing factors, or introduce a location comparison absent from the text. That matches choice D — D is correct.
